evals.prompt: Create a user story for a new Azure Function that listens on a Service Bus queue called 'crew-position-updates', reads the incoming XML payload, transforms it to the JCTE position model, and writes it to the CrewPositions SQL table in JCTE. The function should have retry logic and handle poison messages. Assign it to me.
evals.expected_output: A User Story created in ADO under Crew\Transformers_FTMS_Interface, Crew Backlog iteration, assigned to Jay.Sharma@alaskaair.com, with 5 or 8 story points, and an LLD-template-formatted HTML description covering the Service Bus source, JCTE target, transformation logic, and retry/error handling NFRs.
assertions: The skill shows a preview before creating the work item
The title is concise and action-oriented (verb + noun describing the work)
Story points are in the Fibonacci sequence (1,2,3,5,8,13,21)
The az boards work-item create command includes --area 'Crew\Transformers_FTMS_Interface'
The az boards work-item create command includes --iteration 'Crew\Crew Backlog'
The description HTML includes a Source(s) section mentioning Service Bus or crew-position-updates
The description HTML includes a Target section mentioning JCTE or CrewPositions
The work item is assigned to Jay.Sharma@alaskaair.com
After creation, the output shows the work item ID and a direct URL to view it

evals.prompt: I need a story for building an HTTP trigger API endpoint that exposes crew scheduling data from Qualtero. The APIM base route should be /api/v1/crew/schedule. It reads from Qualtero's ScheduleAssignment and EmployeeShift tables and returns JSON to the caller. This is a pretty straightforward read-only endpoint. Assign to developer Sarah.Jones@alaskaair.com.
evals.expected_output: A User Story with story points of 3 or 5 (simple read API), assigned to Sarah.Jones@alaskaair.com, description includes Source section with Qualtero tables (ScheduleAssignment, EmployeeShift), Integration Method section with HTTP Trigger, APIM baseroute /api/v1/crew/schedule, and Target section showing JSON output.
assertions: The skill shows a preview before creating the work item
Story points are 3 or 5 (not higher — this is a simple read API)
Assigned to Sarah.Jones@alaskaair.com (not the default Jay.Sharma)
The description includes the APIM base route /api/v1/crew/schedule
The description includes the Qualtero source tables: ScheduleAssignment and EmployeeShift
The az boards work-item create command is syntactically correct with all required flags
After creation, the output shows the work item ID and URL

evals.prompt: Create a work item: 'Build complete crew management platform with real-time position tracking, schedule optimization AI, integration with 12 external HR systems, custom reporting dashboards, mobile app, and automated compliance engine'
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes this as an epic (21 story points), shows a preview with 21 pts, warns the user that this should be broken into smaller stories, and either asks for confirmation or suggests splitting before creating.
assertions: The skill estimates 21 story points
The skill includes a warning or recommendation to split this into smaller user stories
The skill still shows a preview before creating anything
The skill does NOT silently create the work item without the user confirming
